message:
fix(infrastructure): close terraform/pulumi global-flags bypass

Same class bug on the infra packs:

- Terraform's `-chdir=<path>` flag goes BEFORE the subcommand —
  every multi-environment org uses it:

    terraform -chdir=./environments/prod destroy -auto-approve
    terraform -chdir=./prod state rm aws_instance.important
    terraform -chdir=./prod workspace delete prod-old
    terraform -chdir=./prod force-unlock abc123

  Each of these escaped the `terraform\s+destroy` / `state\s+rm` /
  `workspace\s+delete` / `force-unlock` rules entirely.

- Pulumi's `--cwd`, `--non-interactive`, `--verbose`, `--logflow`,
  `--profiling` similarly break every `pulumi\s+<sub>` pattern:

    pulumi --cwd ./prod destroy
    pulumi --non-interactive --cwd ./prod up -y
    pulumi --cwd ./prod state delete urn:pulumi:…
    pulumi --verbose --cwd ./prod stack rm prod-old

Apply the uniform `<cli>\b.*?\b<sub>` prefix fix:

- Terraform: 12 safe patterns (plan/init/validate/fmt/show/output/
  state list/state show/graph/version/providers) + 9 destructive
  patterns (destroy, plan -destroy, apply -auto-approve, taint,
  state rm/mv, force-unlock, workspace delete). Safe patterns use
  `\s+<sub>\b` to prevent flag-value bypass (e.g. `-chdir=…plan…`).

- Pulumi: 9 safe patterns (preview/stack ls|select|init/config/
  whoami/version/about/logs) + 6 destructive (destroy, up -y/--yes,
  state delete, stack rm, refresh -y, cancel).

Regression tests `terraform_patterns_match_with_chdir_flag`,
`terraform_safe_patterns_do_not_bypass_via_flag_value`, and
`pulumi_patterns_match_with_global_flags` pin the critical shapes.
Both packs previously had no tests at all.

Full suite: 2,151 passed, 0 failed.
